---
title: "Usage"
---

# Usage guide

The tool offers **three access modes** from source (command line, GUI, web viewer), plus optional [standalone desktop binaries](desktop.qmd) for Linux and Windows.

## Input format

Create a text file (`.txt`) with one **gene symbol, organism** pair per line:

```text
# genes.txt
RAD51,homo_sapiens
TP53,homo_sapiens
DMD,mus_musculus
```

- Blank lines and comments (`#`) are ignored.
- The organism must follow Ensembl naming: `homo_sapiens`, `mus_musculus`, `arabidopsis_thaliana`, etc.
- Duplicates are automatically skipped.

---

## Command line (`main.py`)

### Standard annotation

```bash
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt
```

By default, produces in `results/`:

- `GeneSymbols_2_annotation.html`
- `GeneSymbols_2_annotation.csv`
- `GeneSymbols_2_summary.txt`
- `GeneSymbols_2_manifest.json`

### Main options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--gene GENE,org` | Annotate a single gene without a file |
| `--limit N` | Process only the first N genes |
| `--dry-run` | List genes without calling APIs |
| `--excel` | Add Excel export (`.xlsx`) |
| `--json` | Add structured JSON export |
| `--output-dir DIR` | Custom output directory |
| `--serve` | Open the web viewer when finished |
| `--workers N` | Parallelism (1–4 simultaneous genes) |
| `--no-cache` | Ignore the local cache |
| `--clear-cache` | Clear cache before running |
| `--cache-info` | Show cache statistics |
| `-v` | Verbose logs |
| `--log-file FILE` | Write logs to a file |
| `--html-only` | Generate HTML only |
| `--csv-only` | Generate CSV only |

### Examples

```bash
# Single gene
python3 main.py --gene RAD51,homo_sapiens

# Quick test on 3 genes
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt --limit 3

# All formats + auto-open viewer
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt --excel --json --serve

# Parallel with logs
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt --workers 2 -v --log-file results/run.log

# Full re-annotation (no cache)
python3 main.py data/GeneSymbols_2.txt --no-cache --clear-cache
```

### Console output

At the end of a run, a **coverage report** is printed:

```text
=== Annotation Report ===
Genes analyzed      : 12
Complete genes      : 0
Overall coverage    : 72.3%
From cache / fetched : 10 / 2
Duration            : 45.2s
```

---

## Graphical interface (`interface.py`)

```bash
python3 interface.py
```

The Tkinter GUI lets you:

- Choose a **text file** or enter a **single gene**;
- Set the **output folder** (default: `results/`);
- Toggle cache, HTML, CSV, Excel, JSON;
- Adjust **parallel workers** (1–4);
- Show a **progress bar** during annotation;
- **Open the viewer** automatically after the run.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip
For long gene lists, prefer the command line with `--workers` and `--log-file` — more stable and reproducible.
:::

---

## Web viewer (`viewer.py`)

After an annotation run, launch the local viewer:

```bash
python3 viewer.py --open
```

Default address: `http://127.0.0.1:5000`

### Features

- List of available results in `results/`
- Coverage statistics per run
- Embedded interactive table (iframe)
- CSV, Excel, JSON downloads
- Database ID links open in a **new browser tab** (NCBI, AmiGO, UniProt, etc.)

### Options

```bash
python3 viewer.py --port 8080
python3 viewer.py --dir ./my_run --open
python3 viewer.py --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000   # local network access
```

::: {.callout-note}
## External links
Sites such as NCBI refuse to load *inside* the viewer iframe (`X-Frame-Options`).  
GeneWeave therefore opens every database link in a new tab so the report stays visible.
:::

---

## Desktop binaries

To ship GeneWeave without requiring Python on user machines, see [Desktop App](desktop.qmd):

```bash
bash scripts/build_linux.sh          # → dist/GeneWeave, geneweave-cli, …
scripts\build_windows.bat            # → dist\GeneWeave.exe, …
```

---

## Recommended workflow

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[Prepare genes.txt] --> B{Mode?}
    B -->|CLI| C[python3 main.py genes.txt]
    B -->|GUI| D[python3 interface.py]
    C --> E[results/]
    D --> E
    E --> F[python3 viewer.py --open]
    F --> G[Review coverage and links]
```

1. **Prepare** the input file and validate with `--dry-run`.
2. **Run** annotation (first run: allow several minutes).
3. **Review** the HTML report or web viewer.
4. **Re-run** as needed — the cache speeds up repeated genes.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| NCBI error / rate limit | Reduce `--workers`, increase `NCBI_DELAY` |
| Ensembl timeout / empty Ensembl columns | Retries are automatic (3×, 60s). Re-run the gene or raise `ANNOTATION_HTTP_TIMEOUT` |
| Entire row shows `ERROR: …` and No data found | Older behavior; update and re-run — other databases are kept if one API fails |
| `No data found` everywhere | Check organism format (`homo_sapiens`) |
| GUI won't start | Install `python3-tk` |
| Excel export fails | `pip install openpyxl` |
| Empty viewer | Ensure `results/` contains `*_annotation.html` files |
| Link says site “won't allow the connection” | Reload the viewer; links must open in a **new tab** (see above). Re-run annotation if the HTML was generated with an older build |
| Packaged GUI missing templates | Rebuild with `scripts/build_linux.sh` or `scripts/build_windows.bat` |
